records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Mislaid (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Mislaying.]

defs: To lay in a wrong place; to ascribe to a wrong source.
quotes: The fault is generally mislaid upon nature.

defs: To lay in a place not recollected; to misplace; to lose.
quotes: The . . . charter, indeed, was unfortunately mislaid: and the prayer of their petition was to obtain one of like import in its stead.
